questions.question: What are the primary objectives of this project (e.g., increase sales, improve UX, launch a new feature)?
Who are the target users or customers? What are their key demographics and needs?
What business metrics or KPIs will define success (e.g., conversion rate, retention, load times)?
Is this a new (greenfield) project or an update/migration of an existing system?
If migrating: What are the main reasons for the migration, and what must be preserved or improved from the current version?
Are there existing brand guidelines or style guides (colors, fonts, logos) to follow?
What overall visual style or tone should the UI convey (e.g., corporate, playful, minimalist)?
Do you have reference designs or competitor sites we should consider or avoid?
Will completed design mockups (e.g., Figma files) be provided, or is front-end expected to convert designs into code?
How should key user flows work (e.g., signup, checkout)? Are there mockups or prototypes for these flows?
Are there any mandated design systems or component libraries to use?
Who is responsible for final approval of UI/UX (product owner, marketing team, etc.)?
How should we handle error states, empty states, and loading states in the UI? (e.g., any guidelines for spinners or placeholders)
Are there specific animations or transitions (hover effects, modal popups) required, and should they be adjustable or toggleable (reduced motion)?
Will designers provide final copy/text, or should we use placeholders until content is ready?
Who are the end users (general public, internal users, children, etc.)? Any special needs (e.g., accessibility requirements)?
Are there legal or industry accessibility requirements (WCAG 2.1 level AA, ADA compliance, etc.)?
What accessibility features are mandatory (keyboard navigation, screen reader support)?
Have we conducted or planned an accessibility audit? Who will do it?
Are color contrast and font sizes designed with accessibility in mind? (For example, WCAG recommends a 4.5:1 contrast ratio for normal text[1].)
Are alt text descriptions provided for all images and meaningful icons (improves accessibility and SEO[2])?
Should animations be disabled or reduced for users who prefer reduced motion?
Who on the team will verify accessibility compliance (UX specialist, QA)?
What are the core features and user actions (e.g., user registration, search, shopping cart)? List all key functionality.
Are there any complex UI components required (e.g., data tables, charts, drag-and-drop, rich text editor)?
For e-commerce: which payment gateways, shipping options, and tax rules are needed?
Do we need social logins or external authentication (OAuth providers like Google, Facebook)?
Are there any background processes or real-time features (notifications, live updates)?
What are the priority features (must-have vs. nice-to-have)?
Will there be user roles or permissions affecting the UI (admin vs. regular user screens)?
How should client-side error handling work? For example, what user-friendly messages or retry options should appear on network/API errors?
Are there existing analytics or user behavior data that influence feature priorities?
Will the site use a CMS for content (e.g., Adobe Experience Manager, Magento)?
How is content organized (page templates, components)? Will front-end fetch data from CMS APIs?
Who will supply the initial content (text, images)? Is the content final or subject to change?
Are there images or media with specific format/size requirements? (Consider optimization for web.)
If migrating: How will existing content be migrated to the new system? Will data feeds or scripts be needed?
Are there multi-language versions of content? If so, how are translations managed in the CMS?
Who will update content after launch, and will they need special editing tools?
Which backend services or APIs will the front-end call? (REST endpoints, GraphQL, WebSocket streams, etc.)
Is API documentation available (Swagger/OpenAPI specs, Postman collection)?
Will any APIs require authentication (tokens, cookies, OAuth)? How is this handled?
What data formats and structures do the APIs use (JSON schema)?
Are there rate limits, pagination, or throttling constraints on the APIs?
How are errors returned by the API (error codes, messages)? How should the front-end display them?
If integrating third-party services (e.g., payment, maps, marketing tools): What are the endpoint details, credentials, and workflows for each?
For real-time features: what technologies are in use (WebSockets, Firebase)?
If migrating: Are we keeping existing APIs or building new ones? What changes should we anticipate?
Are there performance targets or budgets (e.g., Lighthouse scores, load times)?
Will the site use server-side rendering (SSR) or static generation for faster initial loads and better SEO?
Are responsive images and lazy loading required for images and media to speed up page loads? (Implementing lazy loading can significantly improve page speed[3].)
Will static assets (images, fonts, JS/CSS) be served via a CDN to reduce latency[3]?
What compression (gzip, Brotli) or bundling strategy is expected for assets?
Is there a need for prefetching or caching strategies (e.g., HTTP caching headers, service workers)?
What is the expected traffic load and user concurrency? Are load-testing benchmarks required?
How will performance be monitored (e.g., RUM tools, synthetic testing)?
Which pages need custom meta tags (titles, descriptions, keywords) for SEO? Who will provide the content?
Will the site use semantic HTML and proper heading hierarchy for SEO?
Do we need to generate XML sitemaps or configure robots.txt for crawlers?
If the site is a SPA (single-page app), how will search engines index it? (SSR or prerendering may be needed.)
Should we implement Open Graph/Twitter Card metadata for social sharing?
Who is responsible for setting up analytics/tracking (Google Analytics, Google Tag Manager, etc.)?
What user actions or events should be tracked (purchases, sign-ups, form submissions)?
Are cookies or tracking pixels subject to user consent (GDPR/CCPA)?
How will we verify analytics integration in testing (e.g., test viewports, debug tools)?
What browsers must be supported (e.g., Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge)? Any specific versions?
Are we supporting legacy browsers (e.g., IE11) or only evergreen browsers?
What devices and screen sizes must the UI accommodate (desktop, mobile, tablet)?
Are responsive breakpoints and layouts specified (e.g., mobile < 480px, tablet 768px+, etc.)?
Has the design been tested for mobile-friendliness? Over 70% of users browse on mobile[4], so responsive design is critical.
For mobile support: is the site optimized for touch interactions and smaller screens?
Are there platform-specific design considerations (e.g., mobile app styles, iOS vs Android navigation)?
How should the UI behave if offline or if network is slow (offline indicators, cached content)?
Which analytics or user-tracking tools will be used (Google Analytics, GA4, Hotjar, etc.)?
Who will provide tracking IDs or tags?
What specific events or conversions need to be tracked (e.g., clicks, sign-ups, purchases)?
Will custom dimensions/metrics or e-commerce tracking be needed in analytics?
How will we test and validate that tracking is correctly firing?
Are there dashboards or reports already set up for this project’s analytics?
What languages and locales must the site/app support? (More than half of users prefer content in their native language[5].)
How will translations be managed and updated (gettext files, JSON, translation service)?
Are there right-to-left (RTL) language requirements? How should layouts adapt?
Are date, time, number, and currency formats different by locale?
Will switching languages require a page reload, or should it be dynamic?
Who will provide translated copy, and what is the workflow for updating it?
What environments exist (development, staging, production)? Who has access to each?
What is the version control strategy (Git repo URL, branching model, CI/CD pipeline)?
Where will the app be hosted (cloud provider, on-prem)? Will Docker or other containers be used?
Who manages DNS, SSL certificates, and domains?
How often will new releases be deployed, and what is the process (manual or automated)?
How will rollbacks or hotfixes be handled if something goes wrong?
For ongoing maintenance, who will support the app post-launch? Who do we contact with issues?
If migrating: Will the old and new systems run in parallel? How and when will cutover happen?
What is the project timeline or target launch date? Are there any hard deadlines?
Are there major milestones (e.g., design freeze, feature complete) we should plan for?
What is the preferred communication channel (email, Slack, project management tool)?
How frequently should status updates or demos be provided?
Who are the key stakeholders (product owner, marketing lead, QA) and decision-makers?
What is the expected turnaround time for feedback on questions or deliverables?
Roles & Responsibilities: Who is responsible for code quality, writing tests, and handling support/bug fixes[6]?
Are there internal coding standards or linters we must adhere to?
How will requirements and changes be documented (spec sheets, tickets, user stories)?
Is there a process for change requests after development has started?
What user data will be collected by the app? How must it be protected?
Are there specific compliance requirements (GDPR, CCPA, HIPAA, PCI) impacting the frontend?
How should sensitive data be handled on the client side (encrypt in storage, use httpOnly cookies)?
What authentication flows are needed (login, logout, registration, password reset, MFA)?
Are there security measures like CAPTCHA on forms or rate-limiting for login attempts?
How should security vulnerabilities be reported and addressed during development?
Is TypeScript required? What strictness level is expected (strict, noImplicitAny, etc.)?
questions.description: TypeScript strict mode catches many bugs early. Confirm the baseline config with the team.

questions.question: What state management approach is preferred? (Redux Toolkit, Zustand, Context API, Jotai, TanStack Query for server state)
questions.description: Misaligned state management choices cause rework. Agree before building the first component.

questions.question: Are there preferred UI component libraries? (shadcn/ui, MUI, Ant Design, Radix, or fully custom)
questions.description: Influences design system, bundle size, and theming strategy.

questions.question: What bundler/build tool is expected? (Webpack, Vite, Turbopack, Parcel)
questions.description: Affects dev server speed, config compatibility, and CI build times.

questions.question: Is there a monorepo setup? (Turborepo, Nx, plain npm/yarn workspaces)
questions.description: Monorepo structure affects how shared packages and apps are developed and deployed.

questions.question: Are there environment variables that must be exposed to the browser (NEXT_PUBLIC_*)? Who owns and manages .env files?
questions.description: Leaked secrets and missing vars are common deployment blockers.

questions.question: What minimum code/test coverage percentage is expected? Which testing frameworks? (Jest, Vitest, Cypress, Playwright)
questions.description: Setting coverage expectations early avoids last-minute scrambles before release.

questions.question: Is there a design token / style dictionary pipeline already in place? Should tokens be auto-synced from Figma?
questions.description: Determines whether CSS variables should come from a generated token file or be written manually.

questions.question: What font family and weight are used for each text style, and what fallback fonts should be applied?
What are the font sizes for headings, subheadings, body text, captions, etc.? Are they fixed or responsive (e.g. using rem or viewport units)?
What are the exact line-height and letter-spacing values for each text style?
Are any text-transformations applied (e.g. uppercase, small-caps, caps)?
Should a given text style map to a specific HTML element (e.g. this title as <H1> vs <H2>)?[2]
Are the text styles based on design-system tokens? If custom, why not use an existing token?
Are placeholder (dummy) text or content lengths considered (for translations or dynamic content)? If not, how to handle overflow/truncation? If text is overflowing how should we break it?
What are the exact color values (hex/RGBA) for all UI elements (text, backgrounds, borders, icons)? Are these defined as design tokens or CSS variables?[1]
Which colors correspond to semantic roles (primary, secondary, success, warning, error, background, surface, etc.)?
Are light/dark mode (or high-contrast) variants defined? If so, what changes (colors, overlays, text colors) when switching themes?
Are hover/active color variants specified for buttons, links, icons, etc.?
Have all text/background color combinations been checked for WCAG contrast compliance? If not, should alternate colors be provided?[6]
Are any gradients, overlays, or shadows used? If so, what are the start/end colors, opacities, and directions?
Are transparent colors or alpha (opacity) values given for overlays or disabled states?
What grid or column system is intended (e.g. 12-column grid, fixed-width container)?
What are the precise margins, padding, and gap values between elements? Are they multiples of a base spacing unit? Should they scale or remain fixed?[7]
Are elements full-width, centered, or fixed-width? For example, what is the maximum container width on desktop?
For 1px borders or hairline strokes: is this supposed to be 1 device pixel on all screens (e.g. a retina hairline) or a fixed CSS pixel?
Are any elements positioned absolutely or using CSS transforms (e.g. overlays, modals)? If so, how are their positions defined?
Should all spacing adhere to a grid or design-token (e.g. 8px, 16px)? If custom values are used, what is the rationale?
Should hidden elements (e.g. off-screen menus) have any specific spacing or is hidden by default?
What screen sizes (mobile, tablet, desktop) are supported? Are explicit breakpoints defined (e.g. 320px, 768px, 1024px)?
Are alternate layouts or mockups provided for smaller screens, or is the design meant to be fluid? How do elements reflow or resize on narrow viewports?
At what width do navigation or multi-column layouts collapse (e.g. switch to hamburger menu or single column)?
Should font sizes or spacing change at certain breakpoints (e.g. larger text on mobile)?
What is the minimum/maximum width or height for this layout or its images? (e.g. if an image scales, what are its min/max dimensions?)[4]
Are device safe-area insets (e.g. notch, status bar) considered on mobile? (E.g. top padding on iOS, Android navigation bar.)
Are any touch-specific behaviors required on mobile (e.g. swipe to open menu)? How should hover-only interactions be handled on touch devices?
For each component (buttons, inputs, cards, etc.), are all states provided: default, hover, focus, active/pressed, disabled, and loading?[8][3]
What is the visual style for each state? (e.g. button hover color, pressed inset, input focus outline, disabled opacity.)
How should empty or placeholder states look? (e.g. empty table, no search results, blank form state)
How should error states appear? (e.g. form validation errors, error messages, styles for invalid fields)
What does a “success” or confirmation state look like (if applicable)?
Do interactive elements have defined focus indicators? For example, what outline or shadow shows when a button or link is focused? (Often covered with hover/focus specs)
If a component toggles (e.g. play/pause, open/closed accordion), are both states in the design?
If a component shows loading (spinner or skeleton), what color and size should it be?
Are these design values (colors, spacing, font sizes, etc.) from an existing design-token library? If custom values are used, should they be added to the token set?[9]
Should these values be implemented as CSS custom properties (variables)? Which ones correspond to light/dark mode tokens or theme variables?
Are there any unit choices to clarify (px vs rem vs %)? (For example, if spacing uses 16px, should we use 1rem=16px or keep px?)
Are measurements given in the design final (or will they change)? If the designer changes a token (e.g. primary color) later, how will that be communicated?
What file formats should we use (SVG for icons, PNG/JPEG for raster)? Are SVGs provided for vector art?[10]
What are the required resolutions of raster images (1x, 2x, 3x)? For example, should we export @2x images for retina?[10]
What is the intended min/max display size of each image/icon? (e.g. should this image scale down to 100px wide or never shrink below 200px?)[4]
Should icons use currentColor (to inherit text color) or fixed fill colors? Are multi-color icons final?
Are all icons and images organized in shared folders or libraries? (Designers often supply a zip of exported assets or keep them in Figma)[11]
For logos/illustrations, should they have transparent background or a fixed color? Are source files (AI, PSD) needed or is SVG sufficient?
If an image is purely decorative, should it have an empty alt (aria-hidden)? For meaningful images, what alt text should be used?[5]
What should happen on user interactions? (For example, does clicking this button open a modal, navigate to a page, submit a form, Scroll etc.?)
Should tooltips, dropdowns or popovers close when clicking outside or pressing ESC?
After an action, what is the resulting state? (E.g. if there is a play button, should it switch to a pause icon upon click?)[12]
Are animations or transitions specified? If so, what easing and duration should be used (e.g. fade in 300ms ease-in-out)?
If elements slide or scroll (e.g. carousels, accordions), should they auto-play or wait for user action?
Are there loading animations (e.g. spinners)? What speed/color are they?
For touch interfaces, are there swipe or gesture interactions?
Are appropriate alt text or ARIA labels provided for all non-text content (icons, images)? If an icon is purely decorative, should it be hidden from screen readers?[5]
Are all form controls and buttons labeled (e.g. using <label> or aria-label)?
Are focus styles defined for all interactive elements (so keyboard users can see focus)?[3]
Do all color contrasts meet WCAG 2.1 AA standards? (If not, what adjustments or alternatives exist?)[6]
Should any dynamic content (like alerts or modals) announce itself to assistive tech (e.g. role="alert")?
Are animations used in a way that could trigger motion sickness? Should prefers-reduced-motion be supported (or provide a non-animated alternative)?
Is RTL (right-to-left) support needed (layout flip, mirrored icons)?
Who will handle exporting assets: designer or developer? If designer provides assets, should they supply a ZIP of images/icons or keep them in Figma?[11]
What formats and resolutions should be exported for each asset? (E.g. SVG for icons; PNG @1x,2x for photos; WebP for web images)[10]
Are font files needed? (If custom fonts are used, how should dev obtain/embed them—Google Fonts link, purchased files, etc.?)
Are images going to be breakpoint specific? Will the image change after certain breakpoints?
How should exported assets be named and organized? (E.g. icon-upload.svg, bg-hero-2x.jpg, etc.)
If using icon sprites or fonts (e.g. FontAwesome), which style/version to use?
Are there any non-UI assets (videos, Lottie animations, PDFs) needed? Where are they?
Which platforms is this design targeting (Web, iOS, Android, Desktop)? Are any platform conventions required (e.g. iOS tab bar height, Android material button style)?
For web vs native: Are there differences in navigation or UI patterns we should follow? (e.g. swipe gestures on mobile vs click on desktop)
Are there any known browser or OS limitations (e.g. older IE, low-end Android) that affect implementation?
On mobile, should touch targets meet a minimum size (e.g. 44pt for iOS) – are all tappable areas large enough?
If this is a desktop app (Electron, etc.), how should window resizing or DPI scaling be handled?
Should we include platform-specific assets (e.g. splash screens, app icons of various sizes)?
After implementation, are all typography, color, and spacing values pixel-perfect as per design?
Have all component states and interactions (hover/focus/active/loading) been implemented and tested?
Are all images/icons correct (no placeholders), optimized, and in the right format/resolution?
Have you verified accessibility requirements (alt text, contrast, focus states) have been met?[6][3]
Is the correct (final) design file being used? If the design is updated, has the dev version been synced?
Are all user flows complete (including error and empty states)?
On each device and browser, does the layout match the design, including responsive breakpoints?
Are there any UI bugs (misaligned items, off-by-one pixels)? (For example: is a 1px border rendering as a hairline? Is text truncating properly?)
Are any translations/localizations complete? If not, do designs accommodate longer text (e.g. menu items, buttons)?
Has the QA process checked for differences from the design (colors, fonts, spacing)? Any discrepancies should be clarified with the designer.
Which API endpoints will the frontend need? (List all expected endpoints for data retrieval and submission.)
Are the APIs RESTful or GraphQL? Are there example queries or schemas?
Is API documentation available (OpenAPI/Swagger specs, Postman collections)? If not, who will provide it?
How will the front-end handle data caching or offline data (if needed)?
How should we handle API versioning? Are multiple API versions to be supported concurrently?
What pagination or filtering parameters do the APIs use?
Are there any bulk data export/import endpoints or admin-specific APIs?
What authentication mechanism is used (session cookies, JWT, OAuth2, SAML, etc.)?
Are there user roles or permissions that affect what data the frontend can fetch?
How should the frontend obtain and store authentication tokens or cookies? (e.g., secure, httpOnly cookies)
Will there be Single Sign-On (SSO) integration? Which identity provider (Okta, Azure AD, etc.)?
Are there multi-factor authentication or CAPTCHA requirements for login?
How should password resets, email confirmations, and user registration be handled?
What databases or storage systems are used (SQL, NoSQL, Elasticsearch, etc.)?
How does the CMS (AEM/Magento) serve content – via APIs or templated pages?
Are there specific caching layers (Redis, CDN, in-memory) to use for frequent data?
How are assets (images, videos) stored and served? (e.g., cloud storage, CDN, AEM DAM)
Are there data synchronization jobs (e.g., importing products from ERP) that affect the frontend?
If product data changes frequently, how is cache invalidation handled?
What third-party services must be integrated (payment gateways, email services, analytics)?
Will you provide API keys/credentials for those services?
Are SDKs or libraries required for these integrations?
What rate limits or quotas exist on third-party APIs?
Are there any licensing or compliance considerations for third-party tools?
Are npm or other libraries restricted (no license, security risk)?
What format do backend errors use? (Consistent JSON structure with codes and messages?)
How should the front-end display different classes of errors (validation errors, server errors, network errors)?
Will there be a global error boundary or error page design to use?
Which logging/monitoring tools are used (Sentry, LogRocket, ELK, etc.)?
How will frontend errors be reported back (are frontend errors logged)?
In case of critical failures, who should be alerted and how?
If migrating: What known bugs or error conditions exist that we should anticipate?
What are the expected traffic patterns (peak users, API call volumes)?
Will we need load balancing or auto-scaling configurations?
How are heavy tasks handled (batch jobs, on-demand scaling)?
Are there SLAs for response times or uptime?
Will the API support gzip or other compression for responses?
How should we handle large datasets (lazy loading, infinite scroll)?
What CORS policies are in place? Do we need to whitelist domains?
What content security policy (CSP) or HTTP headers will the backend enforce?
Are inputs validated and sanitized server-side (to prevent XSS, SQL injection)?
Will the frontend need to integrate with CAPTCHA or other anti-bot measures?
How are security headers (HSTS, X-Frame-Options, etc.) configured?
Are there any audit or penetration test requirements for the frontend?
What environment variables or configuration (API endpoints, feature flags) will the front-end need?
Will the front-end code be deployed via a build pipeline (CI/CD)? Who triggers deployments?
Are there separate dev/staging databases or data mocks to use?
Will we use containerization (Docker) for the front-end?
What Node.js or build tool versions are standardized across environments?
Are there linting and formatting rules enforced on the front-end code?
What testing strategy is expected (unit tests, integration tests, end-to-end tests)?
Are there automated test suites already in place? Which frameworks (Jest, Cypress)?
Is there test data or staging data available for the front-end?
Who is responsible for QA and how are defects managed (Jira, Bugzilla)?
Are acceptance criteria defined for user stories?
How will cross-browser and cross-device testing be handled?
Are load/performance tests planned for critical workflows?
Where is project documentation maintained (wiki, Confluence, code comments)?
What is the policy for documenting new APIs or front-end components?
How are breaking changes communicated to the team?
Will there be a changelog or release notes for users?
Are there code review processes in place? (e.g., pull request requirements)
If this is a migrated project: What is the current backend stack (versions of AEM/Magento, languages)?
What legacy data needs migration, and are there data transformation scripts planned?
Which parts of the old system are being deprecated?
Are there legacy APIs we must support during transition?
Who owns the old codebase and can answer questions about its functionality?
How will we ensure feature parity (or note differences) with the existing system?
